name: Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping
name_original: Piscina natural de Cañamares – circular por el río Escaba y camping La Dehesa
region_intro: Hiking around Cañamares offers diverse landscapes within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. The region features deep forests, impressive ancient geological formations, and captivating river landscapes carved by the Escabas and Júcar rivers. Hikers can explore a variety of terrains, from accessible paths along natural pools to more challenging routes through scenic gorges and unique rock formations. The area is characterized by its natural beauty and varied elevation changes. Best hiking trails around Cañamares The most popular hiking route is El Cucurucho and La Hocecilla Trail, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views and leads through a scenic gorge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cascada Campana del Tío Mil Hombres – circular from Fuertescusa via Cañizar trail (SL-CU 13), a moderate 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This trail features waterfalls and varied forest terrain. Local hikers also love the Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping, a 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail leading through riverside paths and past the natural pool, often completed in about 59 minutes. Hiking around Cañamares is defined by deep forests, unique geological formations, and river landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more strenuous treks. The routes in Cañamares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 170 hikers have used komoot to explore Cañamares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Cañamares?
A: Cañamares offers a wide variety of hiking experiences with over 30 trails. These include 15 easy routes, 15 moderate routes, and 3 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking trails in Cañamares?
A: Yes, Cañamares has several easy trails suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013693821/natural-pool-of-canamares-circular-by-the-escaba-river-and-la-dehesa-camping" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping&lt;/a&gt;. This 3.4 km route takes about an hour and follows riverside paths, passing the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares natural pool&lt;/a&gt;, which is perfect for a refreshing stop.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in the Cañamares area?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in water features. For a hike featuring waterfalls, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013697635/cascada-campana-del-tio-mil-hombres-circular-from-fuertescusa-via-canizar-trail-sl-cu-13" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada Campana del Tío Mil Hombres – circular from Fuertescusa via Cañizar trail (SL-CU 13)&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 6.6 km trail also offers varied forest terrain. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Hocino Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What unique geological formations can I see while hiking in Cañamares?
A: The Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, where Cañamares is located, is renowned for its distinctive geology. You can explore deep gorges carved by rivers like the Escabas and Júcar, and witness impressive ancient rock formations. The "Gate of Hell" (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5650912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta del Infierno&lt;/a&gt;) is a notable tunnel excavated in the rock of the Escabas River, offering a unique sight.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Cañamares?
A: Yes, many trails in Cañamares are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013697635/cascada-campana-del-tio-mil-hombres-circular-from-fuertescusa-via-canizar-trail-sl-cu-13" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada Campana del Tío Mil Hombres – circular from Fuertescusa via Cañizar trail (SL-CU 13)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013693821/natural-pool-of-canamares-circular-by-the-escaba-river-and-la-dehesa-camping" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cañamares?
A: The trails in Cañamares are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from deep forests to captivating river scenery, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or panoramic trails in the area?
A: Yes, several trails offer stunning panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes and gorges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013685249/el-cucurucho-and-la-hocecilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cucurucho and La Hocecilla Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.3 km hike known for its expansive vistas. You can also find great views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3548956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tormo de la Muesca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cañamares?
A: Cañamares is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer is popular, especially for trails that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares natural pool&lt;/a&gt;, where you can cool off. Winters can be cold, but some trails are still accessible for those prepared for cooler weather.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or landmarks to look out for along the trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares natural pool&lt;/a&gt; is a popular spot, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5650912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta del Infierno&lt;/a&gt; (Gate of Hell) is a unique geological feature. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Conde&lt;/a&gt; gorge and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho Bridge&lt;/a&gt; are also notable sights.

Q: What other outdoor activities are available in the Cañamares region besides hiking?
A: The Cañamares area, within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, offers a range of outdoor adventures. Canyoning is popular, particularly in the Hoz Somera canyon. You can also find opportunities for caving, water sports like rafting and kayaking on the rivers, mountain biking, and even horseback riding.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous experience, Cañamares offers difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1242031295/route-of-the-mirador-del-rodenal-pr-cu-42-circular-from-priego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mirador del Rodenal (PR-CU 42) — circular from Priego&lt;/a&gt;, a 14.1 km trail with significant elevation gain, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: Can I find trails that follow rivers or through gorges?
A: The Escabas and Júcar rivers are prominent features of the landscape, carving out scenic gorges. Many trails follow these river courses, offering beautiful riverside walks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Conde&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example of a gorge you can explore.
tips: Wonderful place that you can visit at any time of the year. It is usually more crowded in the summer months since the beach located here is an ideal place to enjoy a refreshing bath in its waters. It also has parking from where you can start your routes.

Camping in the middle of nature in the Sierra de Cuenca, in Cañamares.
Ideal for excursions, disconnect, relax and bathe on the Escabas river beach.
